name: Youth
tagline: Free entry on Wednesdays. Twenty scholarships annually from 2029.
cardBody: Free entry on Wednesdays for under-twenty-fives. Twenty annual scholarships from 2029. Saturday matinees as Mt Kenya University students ask for them.
problem: Young people in Nanyuki between sixteen and twenty-five face two stacked problems: thin disposable income and thin creative supply. The town has a university and two technical colleges; it has no permanent, paid creative jobs to graduate into. Existing nightlife pricing locks under-twenty-fives out of the rooms where local culture is being made.
approach: Downtown is free on Wednesdays for anyone under twenty-five, full stop. Twenty creative scholarships annually from 2029 (a year of tuition at Mt Kenya University plus a Downtown studio placement). Saturday matinees and youth workshops scheduled responsively, not paternalistically: programme what young people ask for.

programmes.name: Free Wednesdays for under-25s
programmes.description: ID at the door, no cover, full programme access. Bar runs as normal so the audience-mixing works.

programmes.name: Downtown creative scholarships
programmes.description: Twenty scholarships annually from 2029. Each covers a year of tuition at Mt Kenya University plus a paid placement inside one of the resident studios for two terms.

programmes.name: Saturday matinees
programmes.description: Daytime programme aimed at under-twenty-fives. Music, dance, and visual showcases by request from the university student union.

programmes.name: Youth music academy (from 2027)
programmes.description: Wednesday afternoons. Free instrumental, vocal, and DJ classes for under-twenty-fives. Drop-in plus a structured term programme.

outcomes.target: Free Wednesdays operational. Average [N] under-25s on the door per week.
Youth music academy launches. Saturday matinees programmed monthly. Partnership conversations with Mt Kenya University underway.
Full scholarship programme live: 20 students placed. First festival youth-week edition.
Scholarship alumni network of [N+]; tracked outcomes on placement, retention, and onward income.

fundingNeeds.label: Free-entry Wednesdays subsidy (door fee + bar margin offset)
Youth academy teaching staff and materials
Youth academy classroom equipment (instruments, decks, recording)

partners: [Partner: Mt Kenya University, tuition agreement and student union]
